tasks.title: Install Vercel AI SDK dependencies and configure environment variables
tasks.description: Install ai@^5.0.0 and @ai-sdk/openai@^1.0.0 packages, configure OpenAI API key and optional environment variables for the LLM-based assistant
tasks.details:
1. Install dependencies:
   - Run: npm install ai@^5.0.0 @ai-sdk/openai@^1.0.0 --workspace=apps/frontend
   - These packages provide the core streaming and tool calling capabilities
   - Zod (^4.0.17) is already installed for schema validation

2. Add environment variables to apps/frontend/.env.local:
   - OPENAI_API_KEY (already exists, verify it's set)
   - OPENAI_MODEL="gpt-4o" (optional, defaults to gpt-4o)
   - CHAT_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE="20" (optional)
   - CHAT_HISTORY_TTL_DAYS="7" (optional)

3. Verify Redis configuration:
   - Check apps/frontend/lib/queue.ts confirms Redis is configured
   - The existing ioredis client can be reused for conversation storage
   - Redis config uses REDIS_HOST and REDIS_PORT env vars

4. Implementation notes:
   - AI SDK 5.0 uses new provider imports: import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' (not 'ai/openai')
   - streamText uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) via Next.js 15 Route Handlers
   - maxSteps parameter enables multi-tool execution (agent behavior)
   - Reference: https://vercel.com/blog/ai-sdk-5
   - Reference: https://ai-sdk.dev/docs/foundations/tools
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify packages appear in apps/frontend/package.json dependencies
2. Confirm OPENAI_API_KEY is accessible via process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
3. Test Redis connection using existing redis client from lib/queue.ts
4. Create test script to import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' and verify no errors
5. Run: npm run build to ensure no compilation errors

tasks.title: Create TypeScript type definitions for chat feature
tasks.description: Define comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for messages, conversations, page context, API requests/responses, and tool execution in a new types/chat.ts file
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify file compiles without TypeScript errors
2. Import types in a test component to ensure proper exports
3. Run: npm run check-types in apps/frontend
4. Validate all interfaces have required fields defined
5. Ensure PageContext matches actual page routes in app directory

tasks.title: Create Redis conversation history manager
tasks.description: Build a conversation history manager using the existing ioredis client to save, load, and manage chat conversations with proper TTL and user isolation
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test createConversation returns valid UUID
2. Test loadConversation returns null for non-existent ID
3. Test saveConversation with >50 messages truncates correctly
4. Verify Redis key format matches pattern: chat:conversation:{userId}:{conversationId}
5. Test TTL is set correctly using redis.ttl(key)
6. Integration test full cycle: create → append → load → delete

tasks.title: Build system prompt generator with context injection
tasks.description: Create a function to generate dynamic system prompts that incorporate user context, capabilities, and instructions for the AI assistant
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test with no context returns base system prompt
2. Test with monitor context includes "Monitor ID: {id}" in prompt
3. Test with incident context includes incident reference instructions
4. Verify prompt length is reasonable (<2000 chars) to avoid token waste
5. Validate all PageType values generate appropriate context text
6. Check prompt formatting is readable and properly structured

tasks.title: Implement core read-only tools (5 tools)
tasks.description: Create 5 essential read-only tools using Vercel AI SDK tool() function with Zod schemas: getMonitors, getMonitorDetails, getMonitorStats, getIncidents, getValidatorStats
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test each tool returns valid JSON structure
2. Verify Zod validation rejects invalid parameters
3. Test authentication by calling tools without sessionId (should fail)
4. Test error handling with non-existent monitorId
5. Verify getMonitorStats accepts all period values (day/week/month)
6. Test getIncidents with different status filters
7. Integration test: call tools via streamText in API route

tasks.title: Create streaming chat API endpoint with rate limiting
tasks.description: Build POST /api/chat route handler using Vercel AI SDK streamText with tool execution, conversation history, and rate limiting (20 msg/min)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test with valid message returns SSE stream (Content-Type: text/event-stream)
2. Test rate limiting: send 21 messages in 60s, verify 21st returns 429
3. Test new conversation creation (no conversationId provided)
4. Test existing conversation loading with valid conversationId
5. Test invalid conversationId returns 404
6. Test message validation: empty string, >2000 chars returns 400
7. Test tool execution by asking "Show me my monitors"
8. Verify onFinish saves messages to Redis
9. Test authentication: call without sessionId returns 401

tasks.title: Create ChatContext provider for page context tracking
tasks.description: Build a React Context provider to track current page type and resource IDs, enabling context-aware assistant responses
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test setContext updates context value
2. Test setContext(null) clears context
3. Test useChatContext throws error when used outside provider
4. Test context persists across re-renders
5. Integration test: set context in page, verify it's accessible in ChatWidget
6. Test cleanup function clears context on unmount

tasks.title: Create useChat custom hook with TanStack Query
tasks.description: Build a custom React hook to manage chat state, send messages, handle streaming responses, and integrate with TanStack Query for optimal state management
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test sendMessage adds user message immediately
2. Test streaming response appends to assistant message
3. Test error handling displays error message
4. Test clearMessages resets state
5. Test stopStreaming aborts request
6. Test conversationId persistence across messages
7. Integration test: send message, verify SSE parsing works
8. Test context injection from useChatContext

tasks.title: Build ChatInput component with textarea and send button
tasks.description: Create a form component with auto-growing textarea, character limit, keyboard shortcuts (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline), and disabled state during streaming
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test Enter key sends message
2. Test Shift+Enter adds newline without sending
3. Test disabled prop disables textarea and button
4. Test character counter updates on input
5. Test messages over 2000 chars show error
6. Test auto-focus on mount
7. Test textarea auto-grows with content
8. Test send button disabled when input is empty/whitespace

tasks.title: Build ChatMessages component for displaying conversation history
tasks.description: Create a scrollable message list component showing user/assistant messages with avatars, timestamps, proper styling, and auto-scroll to latest message
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test empty state shows welcome message and examples
2. Test user messages render right-aligned with User icon
3. Test assistant messages render left-aligned with Bot icon
4. Test timestamps display in HH:mm format
5. Test isStreaming shows typing indicator
6. Test auto-scroll to bottom on new message
7. Test long messages wrap properly (max-w-[80%])
8. Test whitespace-pre-wrap preserves line breaks

tasks.title: Create ChatWidget container component with Sheet UI
tasks.description: Build the main chat widget component that orchestrates ChatInput and ChatMessages, uses Shadcn Sheet for drawer UI, and includes floating button trigger
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test floating button renders in bottom-right corner
2. Test clicking button opens Sheet
3. Test Sheet width is 600px on desktop, full on mobile (<640px)
4. Test Clear button shows confirmation dialog
5. Test Clear button clears messages
6. Test error from useChat shows toast notification
7. Test disabled state during streaming
8. Test Sheet closes on outside click (default behavior)
9. Integration test: send message, verify ChatMessages updates

tasks.title: Integrate ChatWidget and ChatContext into app layout
tasks.description: Add ChatContextProvider wrapper and ChatWidget component to the root layout, making the assistant globally available across all pages
tasks.details:
Update apps/frontend/app/layout.tsx:

```typescript
import { ChatContextProvider } from '@/providers/chat-context-provider';
import { ChatWidget } from '@/components/assistant/chat-widget';

// ... existing imports

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>
      <body className={font.className}>
        <ThemeProvider
          attribute="class"
          defaultTheme="system"
          enableSystem
          disableTransitionOnChange
        >
          <QueryProvider>
            <ChatContextProvider>
              <Toaster />
              {children}
              <ChatWidget />
            </ChatContextProvider>
          </QueryProvider>
        </ThemeProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

Implementation notes:
- ChatContextProvider wraps entire app for global context access
- ChatWidget placed at layout level appears on all pages
- Maintains existing provider hierarchy (Theme → Query → ChatContext)
- ChatWidget is client component, layout can remain server component
- No performance impact (ChatWidget only renders when opened)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify app builds successfully after changes
2. Test ChatWidget floating button appears on all pages
3. Test useChatContext works in any client component
4. Test no hydration errors in console
5. Navigate between pages, verify ChatWidget persists
6. Test conversation state persists across page navigation
7. Verify no layout shift when ChatWidget renders

tasks.title: Add context injection to monitor and incident pages
tasks.description: Inject page context using useChatContext in monitor detail, incident detail, and dashboard pages to enable context-aware assistant responses
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Navigate to monitor detail, verify context.monitorId is set
2. Navigate to incident detail, verify context.incidentId is set
3. Navigate to dashboard, verify context.pageType is 'dashboard'
4. Navigate away, verify context is cleared (null)
5. Integration test: on monitor detail, ask "What's the uptime?", verify assistant uses correct monitor ID
6. Test rapid navigation doesn't cause stale context
7. Check browser console for no errors during navigation

tasks.title: Implement remaining action tools (10 tools)
tasks.description: Add 10 additional tools for creating/updating monitors, managing incidents, escalation policies, and fetching detailed analytics
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test createMonitor with valid params creates monitor
2. Test createMonitor with invalid URL returns error
3. Test updateMonitor ACTIVE→PAUSED works
4. Test getMonitorAnalytics returns geographic data
5. Test updateIncident to 'resolved' status
6. Test createEscalationPolicy with multi-level config
7. Test getValidatorDashboard returns user's validator stats
8. Integration test: ask "Create a monitor for https://example.com" and verify it works end-to-end
9. Test all Zod schemas reject invalid parameters

tasks.title: Add error handling, loading states, and rate limit feedback
tasks.description: Enhance UX with comprehensive error handling, skeleton loaders, rate limit warnings, and retry mechanisms throughout the chat interface
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test rate limit warning appears at 15 messages
2. Test error toast shows on API failure
3. Test retry button reloads page
4. Test stop button cancels streaming
5. Test skeleton loader displays during initial load
6. Test retry logic attempts 2 retries with exponential backoff
7. Test 429 error shows specific rate limit message
8. Test error message appended to chat is user-friendly
9. Integration test: trigger rate limit, verify UX feedback
